data.narrative:
Dream Security, a Series B defense and security company founded by Shalev Hulio and Sebastian Kurz, commands a current post-money valuation of $1.5B following $100M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B).

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. The stage-progression approach, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to Series C transitions, yields an implied valuation of $2.9B. The peer-median method, anchored to five same-sector, same-stage comparables, produces a median implied valuation of $1.0B with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B, reflecting the dispersion observed across the peer set.

Synthesizing these approaches produces a triangulated range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits at the lower-middle bound of this range, suggesting modest upside potential conditional on execution. Key uncertainty stems from stage-progression methodology volatility and the narrow dispersion of late-stage private comparables, which may understate or overstate appropriate market multiples for frontier defense applications.
